_meta.purpose: Canonical palette / typography / illustration tokens for 5 chart-design styles. Hex values were sampled from reference works pixel-by-pixel. Render scripts and style references read from this file.
_meta.fonts_note: Each style lists the original (often commercial) font plus a free fallback. Render scripts should try the listed family and fall back to a generic sans/serif if unavailable.
_meta.comment_output: Charts are rendered as two high-DPI PNGs by default: a complete annotated version and a clean version that removes only readable text/numbers while preserving non-text visuals and data-bearing marks. PPTX output is legacy/optional only when explicitly requested.
_meta.background_policy: Style background tokens are fallback defaults. If an upstream PPT/deck workflow supplies a background plan, inherit that background and adapt contrast, shadows, neutrals, and image prompts accordingly.

illustration.note: Style relies on alpha-overlay and gradient fills, not generated illustration.

illustration.prompt_fragment: flat 2-tone bold-outline editorial vector illustration, palette limited to {recipe_colors}, designed for {background_context_or_recipe_background}, high contrast, risograph / screen-print look, no text, no gradient

high-quality editorial infographic illustration of {generic_object} already transformed by the data: physically sliced, segmented, filled, stacked, or stretched with integrated colored data sections, composed for {background_context_or_fallback_cream}, soft natural shadow adapted to that background, cohesive object-as-chart composition, one consistent material/rendering medium, no text
illustration.guardrail_note: Use only generic, non-branded, non-IP objects; no real identifiable people; original wordplay titles only. Generate the data-bearing object and its colored sections as one integrated image; do not create a clean object that requires flat chart overlays. Avoid mismatched material language such as photoreal object plus watercolor/flat fills. For finance, prefer vault compartments, ledger pages, coin stacks, certificates, or storage drawers over liquid/paint metaphors unless the data is literally about liquidity flow. If a PPT workflow provides a background, design the object edge/shadow for that background instead of forcing cream.

illustration.prompt_fragment: flat vector illustration of {generic_object_or_isometric_scene}, smooth blue-to-purple gradient fill #00A0FA to #5064FA to #6E14AA, white reversed details, soft drop shadow adapted to {background_context_or_fallback_light_gray}, rounded friendly shapes, clean corporate infographic, no text
illustration.use_when: Only when the user requests a custom vector asset or the data concept genuinely needs a custom object/icon. Otherwise render with code-controlled gradient shapes, cards, connectors, and chart marks.

style_5_magazine_monochrome.label: 杂志专题单色信息图
style_5_magazine_monochrome.recipe_formula: Pick 1 dominant hue -> fill whole page -> build levels from its tints/shades -> add 1 cream/neutral -> reserve 1 contrast for title + key numbers.

illustration.prompt_fragment: isometric flat illustration of {generic_pictogram_subject}, monochromatic palette in shades of {dominant} plus one cream tone, subtle shadow adapted to {background_context_or_recipe_background}, editorial magazine infographic style, no text
